content.full_text:
September 15, 2004

Brian T. Hildreth
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-165

Dear Mr. Hildreth:

This letter is to notify you that we have withdrawn the above captioned request for advice on behalf of Oakland Harbor Partners, LLC and Signature Properties, for advice regarding the use of funds provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 We cannot advise you at this time as your request involves past conduct and advice regarding the duties of third parties.

The Commission is unable to provide written advice on issues regarding past conduct. (See section 83114(b); regulation 18329(b)(8)(A)&(C), copy enclosed.) The duties of a "participant" under section 84308 concern the prohibition on the making of contributions either during or for three months following a final decision. You have stated that the contributions have already been made and your question concerns the applicability of section 84308 to state constitutional officers, specifically Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly. We have no indication that Lt. Governor Bustamante or Controller Westly authorized you to request advice on their behalf. Pursuant to regulation 18329(b)(8)(B), written advice or assistance is not provided in response to unauthorized third party requests.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\W-04-165
